strengths: Demonstrated experience with AI-agent infrastructure and developer tooling, including MCP (Model Context Protocol) code generators and frameworks supporting agentic workflows
Strong backend and distributed-systems expertise, including event streaming, microservices, and real-time systems (e.g., high-performance HTTP request handling at Dunnhumby)
Proven track record of scaling engineering teams and establishing technical leadership, evidenced by roles at Optilyz, re:cinq, and RIDE Capital
concerns: Limited direct experience with fintech-specific systems such as real-time credit risk assessment, working-capital management, or embedded finance
No explicit mention of operating multi-agent AI systems in regulated or high-stakes commercial environments with financial consequences
Resume lacks detailed examples of architecting feedback loops for agent learning or compliance/auditability infrastructure for multi-jurisdictional regulations
response.justification: Bogdan Szabo demonstrates strong technical depth in distributed systems, AI-agent infrastructure, and engineering leadership, which aligns well with the agentic AI architecture and engineering leadership responsibilities. However, his lack of explicit fintech domain experience and regulated-environment operations raises concerns about his immediate fit for the fintech-specific requirements of the CTO role.
